text: "Eunuch."... By these are meant, in the spiritual sense, such as are barren in good works. Into the church. That is, into the assembly or congregation of Israel, so as to have the privilege of an Israelite, or to be capable of any place or office among the people of God.
"No uncleanness."... This caution against suffering any filth in the camp was to teach them to fly the filth of sin, which driveth God away from the soul.

"To the stranger."... This was a dispensation granted by God to his people, who, being the Lord of all things, can give a right and title to one upon the goods of another. Otherwise the Scripture everywhere condemns usury as contrary to the law of God, and a crying sin. (See Exod. 22: 25; Lev. 25: 36; 2Esd. 5: 7; Ps. 14:5; Ezec. 18:8).
